title: Syntax error in JavaScript code after upgrade
symptom: After upgrading to the latest version, a syntax error occurs in JavaScript code that previously worked. The error message indicates 'invalid syntax' for the first line of code.
applies_when: Using JavaScript code in a node
not_this_if: Code works without syntax error in previous versions
root_cause: The syntax for defining variables in JavaScript within n8n may have changed with the update. The use of 'const' in the template syntax is not supported, leading to the syntax error.
fix_steps: Add a Set node before the JavaScript code node
Set a key 'responseUrl' with the desired URL value
In the JavaScript code, reference the URL as '{{ $json.responseUrl }}'
agent_instruction: Use a Set node to define 'responseUrl' and reference it in your JavaScript code.
